module.name: require-union-type-alias
module.description: Require inline union types with three or more members and multiple type references to be extracted into named type aliases.

scenarios.name: should allow an inline union of two type references
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the property type annotation
scenarios.then: no violation is reported because two-member unions may remain inline

scenarios.name: should allow a type alias that is itself a union of type references
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the type alias declaration
scenarios.then: no violation is reported because the union is the direct value of a type alias

scenarios.name: should allow a single type reference with undefined
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the property type annotation
scenarios.then: no violation is reported because there is only one type reference member

scenarios.name: should allow keyword-only unions
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the property type annotation
scenarios.then: no violation is reported because there are no type reference members

scenarios.name: should allow literal-only unions
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the type alias declaration
scenarios.then: no violation is reported because literal union members are not type references

scenarios.name: should allow two type references with undefined
scenarios.given: interface IConfig { sourceType: DirectoryImportSourceSelection | SourceType | undefined; }
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the property type annotation
scenarios.then: no violation is reported because undefined does not count toward the union-size threshold

scenarios.name: should allow two type references with null
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the return type annotation
scenarios.then: no violation is reported because null does not count toward the union-size threshold

scenarios.name: should allow a single type reference with null and undefined
scenarios.given: function run(x: TypeA | null | undefined): void {}
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the function parameter type
scenarios.then: no violation is reported because there is only one type reference member

scenarios.name: should report inline union of three type references with undefined in property
scenarios.given: interface IConfig { sourceType: DirectoryImportSourceSelection | SourceType | SourceContext | undefined; }
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the property type annotation
scenarios.then: a violation is reported because the union contains three non-nullish members and multiple type references

scenarios.name: should report inline union of three members in function parameter
scenarios.given: function process(input: TypeA | TypeB | TypeC): void {}
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the parameter type annotation
scenarios.then: a violation is reported advising extraction to a named type alias

scenarios.name: should report inline union of three members in function return type
scenarios.given: function get(): TypeA | TypeB | TypeC | null
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the return type annotation
scenarios.then: a violation is reported advising extraction to a named type alias

scenarios.name: should report inline union of three members in variable declaration
scenarios.given: const x: TypeA | TypeB | TypeC | null = getVal()
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the variable type annotation
scenarios.then: a violation is reported advising extraction to a named type alias

scenarios.name: should report inline union of three members nested inside a generic type argument
scenarios.given: interface IConfig { value: Readonly<TypeA | TypeB | TypeC | undefined>; }
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the union type inside the generic argument
scenarios.then: a violation is reported because the inner union contains multiple type references

scenarios.name: should report inline union of three or more type references
scenarios.given: interface IConfig { value: TypeA | TypeB | TypeC; }
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the property type annotation
scenarios.then: a violation is reported advising extraction to a named type alias

scenarios.name: should report inline union of type references in class property
scenarios.given: class Service { handler: TypeA | TypeB | TypeC | undefined = undefined; }
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the class property type annotation
scenarios.then: a violation is reported advising extraction to a named type alias

scenarios.name: should report inline union of three generic type references
scenarios.given: function handle(items: Array<string> | Set<string> | ReadonlyArray<string>): void {}
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the parameter type annotation
scenarios.then: a violation is reported because Array and Set are type references

scenarios.name: should report inline union of type references in type literal property
scenarios.given: type Config = { source: SourceA | SourceB | SourceC | undefined; }
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the property type annotation inside the type literal
scenarios.then: a violation is reported advising extraction to a named type alias
